req.title: Webhook delivery
req.statement: Outbound webhooks shall retry on 5xx with exponential backoff up to 5 times; permanent failures shall be DLQ'd.

doc.reasoning: The requirement specifies: (1) successful delivery on 2xx – positive/EP; (2) 5xx triggers up to 5 retries with exponential backoff then DLQ on persistent failure – negative/NEG and boundary/BVA at the exact retry limit of 5. State transitions covered are: PENDING→DELIVERED, PENDING→RETRY(x5)→DLQ. A Decision Table test covers the mixed case where early retries fail but the final attempt succeeds, confirming no DLQ is raised when delivery eventually succeeds within the allowed window.

tests.name: test_webhook_delivered_on_first_attempt_200
preconditions: Webhook endpoint is configured for hr-app under /hooks/*
Target URL returns HTTP 200 on first call
A triggering event is ready to be fired
actions: Start a mock HTTP server that always responds 200
Register the webhook pointing at mock server via <register_webhook_fixture>
Poll delivery log until status is DELIVERED or timeout
expected: Webhook is delivered with status DELIVERED
Exactly 1 HTTP call is recorded by mock server
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time
from unittest.mock import patch

def test_webhook_delivered_on_first_attempt_200(
    register_webhook_fixture,
    fire_event_helper,
    mock_http_server,
    delivery_log_client,
    dlq_client,
):
    mock_http_server.set_responses([200])
    hook_id = register_webhook_fixture(url=mock_http_server.url)

    event_id = fire_event_helper(hook_id=hook_id)

    status = delivery_log_client.wait_for_terminal(
        event_id, timeout=10
    )

    assert status == "DELIVERED"
    assert mock_http_server.call_count == 1
    assert dlq_client.contains(event_id) is False

tests.name: test_webhook_dlqd_after_5_consecutive_5xx
preconditions: Webhook endpoint is configured for hr-app under /hooks/*
A triggering event is ready to be fired
actions: Start a mock HTTP server that always responds 500
Register the webhook pointing at mock server via <register_webhook_fixture>
Wait for all retries to exhaust (poll delivery log for DLQ status)
expected: Mock server receives exactly 6 calls (1 initial + 5 retries)
trace: R-WEB-110: 5xx error pathway – DLQ after max retries
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_webhook_dlqd_after_5_consecutive_5xx(
    register_webhook_fixture,
    fire_event_helper,
    mock_http_server,
    delivery_log_client,
    dlq_client,
):
    mock_http_server.set_responses([500] * 6)
    hook_id = register_webhook_fixture(url=mock_http_server.url)

    event_id = fire_event_helper(hook_id=hook_id)

    status = delivery_log_client.wait_for_terminal(
        event_id, timeout=60
    )

    assert status == "FAILED"
    assert mock_http_server.call_count == 6, (
        f"Expected 6 attempts, got {mock_http_server.call_count}"
    )
    assert dlq_client.contains(event_id) is True

tests.name: test_webhook_succeeds_on_5th_retry_no_dlq
preconditions: Webhook endpoint is configured for hr-app under /hooks/*
Target URL returns HTTP 500 for first 5 calls then 200 on the 6th call (5th retry)
A triggering event is ready to be fired
actions: Start a mock HTTP server returning [500,500,500,500,500,200]
Register the webhook pointing at mock server via <register_webhook_fixture>
Poll delivery log until DELIVERED or FAILED terminal state or timeout
expected: No DLQ entry is created – success on boundary attempt prevents DLQ
trace: R-WEB-110: boundary – success on exactly the 5th retry (maximum allowed)
BVA: upper boundary of retry count = 5
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_webhook_succeeds_on_5th_retry_no_dlq(
    register_webhook_fixture,
    fire_event_helper,
    mock_http_server,
    delivery_log_client,
    dlq_client,
):
    # 5 failures then success on attempt 6 (retry #5)
    mock_http_server.set_responses([500, 500, 500, 500, 500, 200])
    hook_id = register_webhook_fixture(url=mock_http_server.url)

    event_id = fire_event_helper(hook_id=hook_id)

    status = delivery_log_client.wait_for_terminal(
        event_id, timeout=120
    )

    assert status == "DELIVERED"
    assert mock_http_server.call_count == 6, (
        f"Expected 6 attempts, got {mock_http_server.call_count}"
    )
    assert dlq_client.contains(event_id) is False

tests.name: test_webhook_retry_state_transitions_with_exponential_backoff
preconditions: Webhook endpoint is configured for hr-app under /hooks/*
Target URL returns 500 for 3 calls then 200
actions: Register webhook and fire triggering event
Collect per-attempt timestamps from delivery log after DELIVERED
expected: State transitions: PENDING -> RETRY_1 -> RETRY_2 -> RETRY_3 -> DELIVERED
Each interval is >= previous interval (exponential growth)
trace: R-WEB-110: state transition coverage of retry states
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_webhook_retry_state_transitions_with_exponential_backoff(
    register_webhook_fixture,
    fire_event_helper,
    mock_http_server,
    delivery_log_client,
    dlq_client,
):
    mock_http_server.set_responses([500, 500, 500, 200])
    hook_id = register_webhook_fixture(url=mock_http_server.url)

    event_id = fire_event_helper(hook_id=hook_id)

    status = delivery_log_client.wait_for_terminal(
        event_id, timeout=60
    )
    assert status == "DELIVERED"

    attempts = delivery_log_client.get_attempts(event_id)
    assert len(attempts) == 4

    intervals = [
        (attempts[i+1]["ts"] - attempts[i]["ts"]).total_seconds()
        for i in range(len(attempts) - 1)
    ]
    for i in range(1, len(intervals)):
        assert intervals[i] >= intervals[i-1], (
            f"Backoff not increasing: {intervals}"
        )
    assert dlq_client.contains(event_id) is False

tests.name: test_webhook_no_retry_on_4xx_response
preconditions: Webhook endpoint is configured for hr-app under /hooks/*
Target URL returns HTTP 400 on first call (client error, non-retriable)
A triggering event is ready to be fired
actions: Start a mock HTTP server returning 400
Register webhook and fire triggering event
Poll delivery log for terminal state
expected: Mock server receives exactly 1 call – no retries for 4xx
trace: R-WEB-110: decision table – 4xx is non-retriable, distinct from 5xx path
DT: response class 4xx -> no retry -> immediate DLQ
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_webhook_no_retry_on_4xx_response(
    register_webhook_fixture,
    fire_event_helper,
    mock_http_server,
    delivery_log_client,
    dlq_client,
):
    mock_http_server.set_responses([400])
    hook_id = register_webhook_fixture(url=mock_http_server.url)

    event_id = fire_event_helper(hook_id=hook_id)

    status = delivery_log_client.wait_for_terminal(
        event_id, timeout=15
    )

    assert mock_http_server.call_count == 1, (
        f"4xx must not be retried, got {mock_http_server.call_count} calls"
    )
    assert status == "FAILED"
    assert dlq_client.contains(event_id) is True
